name: Cu Chulainn, Hound of Ulster
shortDescription: Cu Chulainn is a legendary Irish hero, known for his incredible strength, skill in battle, and youthful beauty. He is the central figure of the Ulster Cycle of Irish mythology.
description: Cu Chulainn, originally named Setanta, is one of the most prominent figures in Irish mythology, particularly within the Ulster Cycle. His birth was prophesied, and his mother was Deichtine, sister of King Conchobar mac Nessa of Ulster. His father is disputed, sometimes said to be the god Lugh. Setanta earned the name Cu Chulainn ('Culann's Hound') after killing the fierce guard dog of the smith Culann in self-defense, vowing to take the dog's place until a replacement could be reared. He trained as a warrior under Scathach, a legendary female warrior, where he mastered many martial skills, including the use of the Gae Bolg, a fearsome barbed spear. Cu Chulainn is known for his 'warp spasm,' a battle frenzy where his appearance transforms into a monstrous figure. He defended Ulster single-handedly against the army of Queen Medb of Connacht in the Táin Bó Cúailnge (Cattle Raid of Cooley), engaging in single combat with various champions. His tragic death came about through a combination of a violation of geasa (taboos) and the combined forces of his enemies.
quests: Defending Ulster against Queen Medb in the Táin Bó Cúailnge
abilities: Mastery of various weapons, especially the spear

sources.title: Táin Bó Cúailnge (The Cattle Raid of Cooley)
sources.author: Unknown

sources.title: Ancient Irish Tales
sources.author: Tom Peete Cross and Clark Harris Slover
